title: Platform sports catalog — seed data
description: The 30+ sport baseline shipped by the platform. Loaded on first-boot via SeedPlatformSportsCatalogJob (tenant_id NULL, is_platform_seeded=true for every row). Every downstream tenant reads this catalog as read-only reference data; Enterprise tenants may extend it with customs. The seeder is IDEMPOTENT — running it against a JSON snapshot that matches the DB state is a no-op. On explicit reseed with --force, the seeder UPSERTs new rows, updates existing rows, and DEACTIVATES rows that were removed from the JSON (never hard-deletes).
notes: Slugs use lowercase snake_case; parent-prefixed for descendants (`football_11v11`, `football_goalkeeper`). This makes them self-describing when read out of context (an enrollment carrying `sport_key='football_goalkeeper'` would fail resolver validation — the position slug goes in the position_key column).

The name field is English-only in this seed; the localization module's HasTranslations trait can populate other locales via the seed extension (Arabic, French, Spanish, ...). Consumers holding a Sport model read `$sport->name` which returns the caller's locale automatically.

gender_typical defaults to 'any' when omitted. Some sports (e.g. netball is typically female-dominant, though not exclusively) leave it 'any' rather than 'female_only' — the field is advisory + should not lock out participation.

The `governing_body` field cites the primary international federation for reference. Downstream tenants may add tenant-custom Sport rows with different governing_body values when they follow a national/regional federation.
Icons reference Iconify (`mdi:*`, `game-icons:*`). The frontend resolves them via the Iconify CDN at render time.
player_count_min / player_count_max on Discipline are advisory — they shape defaults in the Team creation UI but do NOT enforce roster size at write time (downstream Team module handles enforcement).

The positions list per Sport / Discipline is a REPRESENTATIVE seed, not exhaustive. Tenants that need specialised positions (e.g. American football has dozens more position variants) can add customs on Enterprise.

sports.name: Football (Soccer)
sports.description: Association football / soccer. The world's most popular team sport.

disciplines.name: 11-a-side Football
disciplines.description: Standard 11-player format on a full-size pitch. 90-minute matches divided into two 45-minute halves.

disciplines.name: Futsal
disciplines.description: 5-a-side indoor variant with smaller ball + hard court + no rebound walls.

disciplines.name: 5-a-side Football
disciplines.description: Small-sided recreational format. Common at community centers + youth academies.

disciplines.name: 7-a-side Football (Youth)
disciplines.description: Youth-development format. Common for U8-U11 in Europe + North America.

sports.name: Basketball
sports.description: 5-on-5 hoop-and-ball court sport. Second-most popular team sport globally.

disciplines.name: FIBA 5v5
disciplines.description: Standard 5-player format. Four 10-minute quarters (FIBA) or 12-minute quarters (NBA).

disciplines.name: 3x3 Streetball
disciplines.description: FIBA 3x3 half-court format. First to 21 points OR 10 minutes.

sports.name: Rugby Union
sports.description: 15-a-side full-contact ball sport.

sports.name: American Football
sports.description: 11-a-side gridiron football.

disciplines.name: Flag Football
disciplines.description: Non-contact variant with belt-flag tackles. 5-9 players per side.

sports.name: Cricket
sports.description: 11-a-side bat-and-ball sport.

disciplines.name: Test Cricket
disciplines.description: Multi-day format (up to 5 days). Two innings per side.

sports.name: Baseball
sports.description: 9-a-side bat-and-ball sport played on a diamond.

sports.name: Field Hockey
sports.description: 11-a-side stick-and-ball sport on grass or turf.

sports.name: Ice Hockey
sports.description: 6-a-side stick-and-puck sport on ice.

sports.name: Volleyball
sports.description: 6-a-side net-and-ball sport.

disciplines.name: Indoor Volleyball
disciplines.description: 6-a-side indoor. Best of 5 sets to 25 points.

sports.name: Handball
sports.description: 7-a-side indoor throw-and-catch goal sport.

sports.name: Netball
sports.description: 7-a-side ball-through-hoop sport, Commonwealth heritage.

sports.name: Lacrosse
sports.description: 10-a-side stick-and-ball sport with a netted stick.

sports.name: Swimming
sports.description: Individual + relay aquatic racing across four canonical strokes.

disciplines.name: Individual Medley
disciplines.description: All four strokes in one race (butterfly → backstroke → breaststroke → freestyle).

sports.name: Water Polo
sports.description: 7-a-side ball sport played in a pool.

sports.name: Tennis
sports.description: Singles + doubles racquet sport played on grass, clay, or hard court.

sports.name: Badminton
sports.description: Indoor racquet sport with a shuttlecock.

sports.name: Squash
sports.description: Indoor racquet sport played in a four-walled court.

sports.name: Table Tennis
sports.description: Indoor small-ball racquet sport played on a table.

sports.name: Gymnastics
sports.description: Artistic + rhythmic gymnastics disciplines.

disciplines.name: Artistic Gymnastics
disciplines.description: Apparatus-based routines (floor, beam, bars, vault, rings, pommel horse).

disciplines.name: Rhythmic Gymnastics
disciplines.description: Apparatus-based routines with hoop, ball, ribbon, clubs, rope.

sports.name: Athletics (Track & Field)
sports.description: Running, jumping, throwing, and combined-event disciplines.

disciplines.name: Jumps
disciplines.description: High jump, long jump, triple jump, pole vault.

sports.name: Cycling
sports.description: Road, track, mountain-bike, and BMX cycling.

sports.name: Triathlon
sports.description: Multi-sport race: swim + bike + run.

sports.name: Martial Arts
sports.description: Umbrella sport covering karate, taekwondo, judo, BJJ, and other styles.

disciplines.name: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu
disciplines.description: Ground-fighting grappling art focused on submissions.

sports.name: Boxing
sports.description: Amateur + professional boxing.

sports.name: Wrestling
sports.description: Greco-Roman + freestyle wrestling.

sports.name: Fencing
sports.description: Sword-based combat sport with three weapon disciplines.

sports.name: Skiing
sports.description: Alpine + nordic + freestyle skiing.

sports.name: Snowboarding
sports.description: Alpine + freestyle snowboarding.

sports.name: Figure Skating
sports.description: Ice-based routines with jumps, spins, and choreography.

sports.name: Yoga
sports.description: Physical + breathing + meditation practice.

disciplines.name: Vinyasa Yoga
disciplines.description: Flow-based style — poses linked by breath.

sports.name: Pilates
sports.description: Low-impact strength + flexibility method.

sports.name: General Fitness
sports.description: Umbrella sport for gym-based strength + cardio training that doesn't fit a specific sport taxonomy.

disciplines.name: Strength Training
disciplines.description: Resistance training with weights or machines.

sports.name: CrossFit
sports.description: High-intensity constantly-varied functional-movement training.

sports.name: Dance
sports.description: Umbrella for competitive + recreational dance styles.

sports.name: Aerobics
sports.description: Cardiovascular group-exercise format.

sports.name: Chess
sports.description: Two-player strategy board game.

sports.name: Esports
sports.description: Competitive video-game play across multiple game titles.

disciplines.name: MOBA
disciplines.description: Multiplayer Online Battle Arena (League of Legends, Dota 2).

sports.name: General Recreation
sports.description: Umbrella sport for programmes that don't fit a specific sport taxonomy (mixed-activity summer camps, general PE, adventure-play).
